fgr — Factorio DSL compiler report

A high-level production graph compiles to a real Factorio layout; a generator-agnostic verifier traces material & fluid flow to confirm it realizes the spec; FBSR renders the result. Correctness comes from the verifier, not the picture.

6/6
basic examples
6/6
complex factories
31/37
generated stress DAGs
37/43
stress battery total

How it works

.fgr DSL ──parse──▶ Graph(spec) ──generate──▶ Layout ──verify──▶ PASS/FAIL ──▶ FBSR ──▶ PNG

Lanes: A -> B dedicated belt · A -> B, C one belt split to many (splitter bus) · A, B -> C merge · A ~> B fluid lane (pipe). Machines: input/output chests, assembler, furnace, chemical plant, fluid source (pipes attach at real fluid-box tiles).

Verifier model vs. Factorio data (FBSR)

[ok ] inserter picks at +direction, drops at -direction (all orientations) — Factorio base pickup=[0, -1], insert=[0, 1.2]
[ok ] real verify() agrees with Factorio inserter flow (probe, all dirs)
[ok ] entity footprints match the SIZE table — checked assembling-machine-2, chemical-plant, electric-furnace, infinity-chest, infinity-pipe, inserter, pipe, pipe-to-ground, splitter, steel-chest, storage-tank, transport-belt, underground-belt
[ok ] pipe-to-ground tunnels opposite its open mouth — open dir=0, underground dir=8
[ok ] chemical-plant fluid boxes match Factorio data — [((0, -1), 'input'), ((0, 3), 'output'), ((2, -1), 'input'), ((2, 3), 'output')]
[ok ] assembling-machine-2 fluid boxes match Factorio data — [((1, -1), 'input'), ((1, 3), 'output')]

Complex factories

Hand-authored multi-step builds: deep chains, reconvergence, high fan-in, furnaces, and oil/chemical fluids (pipes, tanks, mixing-free networks).

deep_linear PASS

11 nodes · 10 lanes · 115 entities · 131x3 tiles · 1 ms
# A long dependency chain (depth ~10); steel-plate is smelted in a furnace.
input iron : iron-plate
assembler a1 : iron-gear-wheel
assembler a2 : pipe
assembler a3 : engine-unit
furnace a4 : steel-plate
assembler a5 : iron-stick
assembler a6 : rail
assembler a7 : electric-furnace
assembler a8 : productivity-module
assembler a9 : production-science-pack
output out
iron -> a1 -> a2 -> a3 -> a4 -> a5 -> a6 -> a7 -> a8 -> a9 -> out
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes

flying_robot_frame PASS

14 nodes · 20 lanes · 503 entities · 67x36 tiles · 164 ms
# Deep multi-step with smelting (furnace) and fluids (pipes): flying-robot-frame.
input iron : iron-plate
input copper : copper-plate
fluid lube : lubricant
fluid acid : sulfuric-acid
furnace steel : steel-plate
assembler gear : iron-gear-wheel
assembler pipe_part : pipe
assembler cable : copper-cable
assembler green : electronic-circuit
assembler engine : engine-unit
assembler eengine : electric-engine-unit   # crafting-with-fluid (lubricant) -> assembler, not chemical
chemical battery : battery                  # battery is a chemistry recipe -> chemical plant
assembler frame : flying-robot-frame
output frame_out
iron -> steel, gear, pipe_part, green, battery
copper -> cable, battery
cable -> green
gear -> engine
pipe_part -> engine
steel -> engine, frame
engine -> eengine
green -> eengine, frame
lube ~> eengine
acid ~> battery
eengine -> frame
battery -> frame
frame -> frame_out
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes
[ok ] every fluid lane connects at a real fluid-box (pipe network)
[ok ] no fluid mixing (one fluid per pipe network)

processing_unit PASS

9 nodes · 10 lanes · 269 entities · 67x18 tiles · 62 ms
# Deep electronics: copper-plate -> cable -> green -> red -> blue (processing-unit).
# Reconvergent (cable->green,red; green->red,blue). Sulfuric acid is a FLUID (~>).
input iron : iron-plate
input copper : copper-plate
input plastic : plastic-bar
fluid acid : sulfuric-acid
assembler cable : copper-cable
assembler green : electronic-circuit
assembler red : advanced-circuit
assembler blue : processing-unit   # processing-unit needs sulfuric-acid (crafting-with-fluid) -> assembler
output chips
copper -> cable
cable -> green, red
iron -> green
green -> red, blue
plastic -> red
red -> blue
acid ~> blue
blue -> chips
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once

[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds

[ok ] no fluid mixing (one fluid per pipe network)

sulfuric_acid PASS

7 nodes · 6 lanes · 108 entities · 30x14 tiles · 5 ms
# Sulfuric acid: a chemical-plant takes water (FLUID, ~>) + iron + sulfur (items).
# Steel is smelted in a FURNACE. Items go to a chest, acid is piped to a tank.
input iron : iron-plate
input sulfur : sulfur
furnace steel : steel-plate
fluid water : water
chemical acid : sulfuric-acid
output steel_out
output acid_tank
iron -> steel
iron -> acid
sulfur -> acid
water ~> acid
steel -> steel_out
acid ~> acid_tank

assembler green : electronic-circuit

assembler c7 : lab
output out
copper -> cable
cable -> green
iron -> green
green -> c0, c1, c2, c3, c4, c5, c6, c7
c0 -> out
c1 -> out
c2 -> out
c3 -> out
c4 -> out
c5 -> out
c6 -> out
c7 -> out

Basic examples

bus PASS

6 nodes · 5 lanes · 81 entities · 27x15 tiles · 2 ms
# One belt feeding several consumers, via splitters.
#
#                         .-> assembler gears   -> out_gears
#   iron --(one belt)----+--> assembler sticks  -> out_sticks
#                         '-> output  out_raw
#
# `iron -> gears, sticks, out_raw` is a SINGLE output belt off the iron chest; the
# compiler builds a splitter chain that peels one branch per consumer. Contrast
# with writing three separate `iron -> ...` lines, which would demand three output
# inserters on the (1x1) iron chest — impossible. The shared belt needs only one.

input     iron    : iron-plate
assembler gears   : iron-gear-wheel
assembler sticks  : iron-stick
output    out_gears
output    out_sticks
output    out_raw

iron  -> gears, sticks, out_raw
gears  -> out_gears
sticks -> out_sticks
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes

circuits PASS

5 nodes · 4 lanes · 70 entities · 40x7 tiles · 3 ms
# A two-stage factory with a multi-input assembler.
#
# copper-plate -> copper-cable -.
#                                +-> electronic-circuit -> output
# iron-plate -------------------'
#
# `circuit` pulls TWO lanes (iron + cable); the compiler must give it two input
# inserters on its west side and route both belt lanes without collisions. The
# iron lane also has to travel PAST the cable column to reach the circuit column.

input     copper  : copper-plate
input     iron    : iron-plate
assembler cable   : copper-cable
assembler circuit : electronic-circuit
output    chips

copper -> cable
cable  -> circuit
iron   -> circuit
circuit -> chips
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes

fanout PASS

4 nodes · 3 lanes · 41 entities · 27x6 tiles · 1 ms
# Fan-out: ONE belt off the cable assembler feeds two consumers (via a splitter).
#
#                       .-> output spool_a
#   copper -> cable ---+   (single shared belt, splitter bus)
#                       '-> output spool_b
#
# `cable -> spool_a, spool_b` is one output belt; the compiler runs a south-flowing
# trunk off the assembler and a splitter peels each consumer off it.

input     copper : copper-plate
assembler cable  : copper-cable
output    spool_a
output    spool_b

copper -> cable
cable  -> spool_a, spool_b
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes

gears PASS

3 nodes · 2 lanes · 27 entities · 27x3 tiles · 1 ms
# The "hello world" factory: turn iron plates into gears, ship the gears out.
#
#   input chest --belt--> assembler (iron-gear-wheel) --belt--> output chest
#
# We declare WHAT the factory is; the compiler decides WHERE everything goes
# (assembler position, inserters, and the actual belt tile paths) and the
# verifier confirms the placed layout really carries items along each lane.

input     iron  : iron-plate
assembler gears : iron-gear-wheel
output    out

iron  -> gears
gears -> out
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes

merge PASS

4 nodes · 3 lanes · 42 entities · 27x7 tiles · 1 ms
# Merge: two sources onto ONE belt, via a splitter.
#
#   iron_a --.
#            +--(one merged belt)--> assembler gears -> out
#   iron_b --'
#
# `iron_a, iron_b -> gears` is the mirror of fan-out: each source gets its own
# output inserter, a splitter combines both onto a single belt, and `gears` is fed
# by just ONE input inserter. (A splitter merges as well as it splits.)

input     iron_a : iron-plate
input     iron_b : iron-plate
assembler gears  : iron-gear-wheel
output    out

iron_a, iron_b -> gears
gears -> out
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes

science PASS

5 nodes · 4 lanes · 70 entities · 40x7 tiles · 3 ms
# A deeper, recipe-correct chain: red science (automation-science-pack).
#
#   iron-plate -> iron-gear-wheel --.
#                                    +-> automation-science-pack -> output
#   copper-plate -------------------'
#
# automation-science-pack really is 1 gear + 1 copper plate, so this is a valid
# factory. Shows a 3-deep graph and a multi-input final assembler.

input     iron    : iron-plate
input     copper  : copper-plate
assembler gears   : iron-gear-wheel
assembler science : automation-science-pack
output    out

iron   -> gears
gears  -> science
copper -> science
science -> out
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes

Stress battery — generated DAGs

37 machine-generated complex recipe graphs run through compile + verify to find where the generator breaks. All failures are the heuristic router/manifold hitting its limit (extreme single-source fan-outs, or large dense graphs that exhaust the rip-up budget) — not the verifier. See STATUS.md.

Showcase — largest passing stress graphs

scale_1 PASS

33 nodes · 57 lanes · 1906 entities · 104x60 tiles · 7021 ms
# scale_1: rocket-part megafactory.
# Oil refining -> plastics/sulfur/lube/fuel, a full electronics tower,
# smelting, mechanicals, and final rocket-part + robot assembly.
# Deep (8 layers) + reconvergent + fluid branching.

input iron : iron-plate
input copper : copper-plate
input coal : coal
input stone : stone
fluid crude : crude-oil
fluid water : water

furnace steel : steel-plate
furnace brick : stone-brick

chemical oil : lubricant
chemical lube : lubricant
chemical plastic : plastic-bar
chemical sulfur : sulfur
chemical solidfuel : solid-fuel-from-light-oil
chemical acid : sulfuric-acid
assembler rocketfuel : rocket-fuel
chemical battery : battery
assembler procunit : processing-unit
assembler eengine : electric-engine-unit

assembler cable : copper-cable
assembler gear : iron-gear-wheel
assembler pipe_part : pipe
assembler green : electronic-circuit
assembler red : advanced-circuit
assembler engine : engine-unit
assembler efurnace : electric-furnace
assembler speedmod : speed-module
assembler lds : low-density-structure
assembler rcu : engine-unit
assembler frame : flying-robot-frame
assembler consrobot : construction-robot
assembler rocketpart : low-density-structure

output out
output bots_out

# --- fluids (pipes) ---
crude ~> oil
water ~> oil, sulfur, acid
oil ~> lube, plastic, sulfur, solidfuel, rocketfuel
lube ~> eengine
acid ~> battery, procunit

# --- smelting + base item distribution ---
iron -> steel, gear, pipe_part, green, acid, battery
copper -> cable, battery, lds
coal -> plastic
stone -> brick

# --- electronics tower ---
cable -> green, red
green -> red, procunit, eengine, speedmod, frame, consrobot
plastic -> red, lds
red -> procunit, efurnace, speedmod
sulfur -> acid

# --- mechanicals ---
steel -> engine, efurnace, lds, frame
gear -> engine
pipe_part -> engine
brick -> efurnace
engine -> eengine
solidfuel -> rocketfuel

# --- top assemblies ---
procunit -> rcu
speedmod -> rcu
battery -> frame
eengine -> frame
rcu -> rocketpart
lds -> rocketpart
rocketfuel -> rocketpart
frame -> consrobot

# --- outputs ---
rocketpart -> out
consrobot -> bots_out
efurnace -> bots_out
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes
[ok ] every fluid lane connects at a real fluid-box (pipe network)
[ok ] no fluid mixing (one fluid per pipe network)

scale_3 PASS

28 nodes · 42 lanes · 1238 entities · 95x48 tiles · 1637 ms
# scale_3: oil & chemical refinery complex (fluid-heavy).
# Two parallel refineries, heavy/light cracking, then sulfur/acid/plastic/
# battery/explosives/rocket-fuel. Lots of branching pipes + fluid tanks.

input coal : coal
input iron : iron-plate
input copper : copper-plate
fluid crude : crude-oil
fluid water : water

chemical oil1 : lubricant
chemical oil2 : lubricant
chemical crackH : heavy-oil-cracking
chemical crackL : light-oil-cracking
chemical lube : lubricant
chemical solid1 : solid-fuel-from-light-oil
chemical solid2 : solid-fuel-from-light-oil
chemical plastic : plastic-bar
chemical sulfur : sulfur
chemical acid : sulfuric-acid
chemical explosives : explosives
chemical battery : battery
assembler rocketfuel : rocket-fuel
assembler procunit : processing-unit

assembler cable : copper-cable
assembler green : electronic-circuit
assembler red : advanced-circuit

output lube_tank
output acid_tank
output battery_out
output chips_out
output explosives_out
output fuel_out

# --- fluids (pipes), parallel refineries + cracking ---
crude ~> oil1, oil2
water ~> oil1, oil2, crackH, crackL, sulfur, acid, explosives
oil1 ~> crackH, lube, plastic, sulfur
oil2 ~> solid1, rocketfuel
crackH ~> crackL, solid2
crackL ~> plastic
lube ~> lube_tank
acid ~> battery, procunit, acid_tank

# --- item lanes ---
coal -> plastic, explosives
iron -> acid, battery, green
copper -> cable, battery
cable -> green
green -> procunit, red
red -> procunit
sulfur -> acid, explosives
solid1 -> rocketfuel
solid2 -> rocketfuel
plastic -> red

# --- outputs ---
battery -> battery_out
procunit -> chips_out
explosives -> explosives_out
rocketfuel -> fuel_out
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes
[ok ] every fluid lane connects at a real fluid-box (pipe network)
[ok ] no fluid mixing (one fluid per pipe network)

deepchain_6 PASS

23 nodes · 28 lanes · 1137 entities · 147x35 tiles · 122 ms
# deepchain_6: a "build a rocket" progression spine (depth 12) from ore through
# engines, robot frames, low density structure and a rocket control unit to a
# rocket part. Fluids (lubricant, light oil) and an electronics branch feed in.

input     iron_ore     : iron-ore
input     copper       : copper-plate
input     steel_in     : steel-plate
input     plastic      : plastic-bar
fluid     lube         : lubricant
fluid     light_oil    : light-oil
fluid     acid         : sulfuric-acid

furnace   iron_plate   : iron-plate
assembler gear         : iron-gear-wheel
assembler pipe_part    : pipe
assembler engine       : engine-unit
assembler  eengine      : electric-engine-unit
assembler frame        : flying-robot-frame
assembler lds          : low-density-structure
assembler rcu          : engine-unit
assembler rocket_fuel  : rocket-fuel
assembler rocket_part  : low-density-structure
output    parts_out

# electronics branch
assembler cable        : copper-cable
assembler green        : electronic-circuit
assembler red          : advanced-circuit
assembler  blue         : processing-unit
# solid fuel branch
chemical  solid_fuel   : solid-fuel-from-light-oil

# --- the deep spine (depth 12) ---
iron_ore -> iron_plate -> gear -> pipe_part -> engine -> eengine -> frame -> lds -> rcu -> rocket_fuel -> rocket_part -> parts_out

# --- electric engine: lubricant (fluid) + green circuit ---
lube ~> eengine
green -> eengine

# --- electronics branch with long lanes into the spine ---
copper -> cable -> green -> red -> blue
plastic -> red
green -> blue
acid ~> blue
blue -> rcu

# --- low density structure ingredients ---
steel_in -> lds
copper -> lds
plastic -> lds

# --- flying robot frame extras ---
steel_in -> frame
green -> frame

# --- rocket fuel from solid fuel (light oil -> solid fuel) ---
light_oil ~> solid_fuel
solid_fuel -> rocket_fuel
[ok ] no overlapping entities
[ok ] every node placed exactly once
[ok ] node protos/recipes/items match spec
[ok ] no dangling inserters / unpaired undergrounds
[ok ] every declared belt lane physically connects
[ok ] no undeclared belt lanes
[ok ] every fluid lane connects at a real fluid-box (pipe network)
[ok ] no fluid mixing (one fluid per pipe network)

reconverge_2 PASS

26 nodes · 40 lanes · 1137 entities · 69x62 tiles · 1206 ms
# Full science suite: red + green + military + chemical packs. iron, gears,
# electronic-circuit, copper, steel are shared across many sub-assemblies at
# varied depth. sulfur is a chemical-plant recipe (water + petroleum-gas fluids).
input iron : iron-plate
input copper : copper-plate
input coal : coal
input stone_brick : stone-brick
input plastic : plastic-bar
fluid water : water
fluid petro : petroleum-gas
furnace steel : steel-plate
assembler gear : iron-gear-wheel
assembler cable : copper-cable
assembler green : electronic-circuit
assembler red_circ : advanced-circuit
assembler pipe_part : pipe
assembler belt : transport-belt
assembler arm : inserter
assembler engine : engine-unit
assembler mag : firearm-magazine
assembler pmag : piercing-rounds-magazine
assembler grenade : grenade
assembler wall : stone-wall
chemical sulfur : sulfur
assembler redsci : automation-science-pack
assembler greensci : logistic-science-pack
assembler milsci : military-science-pack
assembler chemsci : chemical-science-pack
output sci_out

iron -> gear, green, steel, pipe_part, belt, arm, mag, grenade
copper -> cable, redsci, pmag
gear -> belt, arm, redsci, engine
cable -> green, red_circ
green -> arm, red_circ
steel -> pmag, engine
plastic -> red_circ
coal -> grenade
stone_brick -> wall
pipe_part -> engine
mag -> pmag
engine -> chemsci
red_circ -> chemsci
sulfur -> chemsci
pmag -> milsci
grenade -> milsci
wall -> milsci
belt -> greensci
arm -> greensci
water ~> sulfur
petro ~> sulfur
redsci, greensci, milsci, chemsci -> sci_out
